objective: Test an expanded mixed-hook source for rpf50 support and record whether it solves the Wave 55 source-capacity blocker.
non_negotiables: The existing mixed-hook exact shard commands must remain stable.
The expanded source is diagnostic-only unless it reaches rpf50 support and then passes materialization, replay, split, projection, and baseline gates.
Any support increase below rpf50 must be recorded as a no-go rather than promoted.
Source-capacity claims must report unique left/right component value counts and selected rows by topology.

tasks.prompt: Add an expanded mixed-hook support diagnostic command and test whether it clears rpf50.
acceptance: The new diagnostic command is separate from the existing mixed-hook exact shard command.
rpf1 regression reports 125 left and 226 right unique component value digests.
rpf50 report is recorded and interpreted as no-go unless it reaches 150 selected rows.

tasks.prompt: Record Wave 56 as a source-expansion no-go and route the next wave to a stronger source family or split/ablation design.
commands: python3 -m py_compile engine/ml_model.py engine/test.py agents/network.py agents/label_schema.py
acceptance: Docs state that expanded mixed hooks improve selected rpf50 rows from 109 to 112 but remain below 150.
Docs route next work to materially new source families or stronger ablations.

integration_gates.command: python3 agents/network.py validate && python3 agents/network.py validate-wave agents/waves/wave_56_expanded_mixed_hook_source_no_go.json
integration_gates.success_signal: The Wave 56 plan is internally consistent.
The expanded mixed-hook report is well-formed.
